chunk_summary: Guidelines for preparing F-75 and F-100 therapeutic feeds with electrolyte fortification for managing severe acute malnutrition, emphasizing safety, hygiene, and proper supplementation.
learning_objectives: Understand the preparation of F-75 and F-100 therapeutic feeds for SAM management.
Learn the importance of electrolyte fortification, including potassium and magnesium supplementation.
Identify practical ward checks to ensure safe and effective feeding practices.
Recognize hygiene and process issues in feed preparation to prevent complications like diarrhea.
key_facts: F-75 is a starter diet made from milk, sugar, oil, and water; F-100 is a catch-up diet with higher milk content.
Electrolyte fortification requires added potassium and magnesium, with restricted sodium.
Feeds must be prepared with clean utensils, boiled or safe water, and discarded if kept beyond protocol time.
Double-check feed volume calculations against weight to prevent errors.
Night feeds must be delivered, and feeds should be warmed before administration.
Diarrhea after feed changes often indicates hygiene or mixing issues.
reference_quote: Therapeutic feeding safety depends on correct preparation and mineral composition, not only calories. (National Health Mission, Participant manual for facility-based care of severe acute malnutrition)
document_key_topics: Hygiene and safety in therapeutic feeding
